QF.id: Role of RTAs in Mutual Fund Transactions_35

question.text: Vikram submitted his address change request at an ISC operated by an RTA. This is classified as:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Address changes, name changes, bank mandate updates, signature changes, and nomination updates are all classified as non-financial transactions. They modify investor records but do not involve movement of money or units. **The Trap:** Some non-financial transactions like bank mandate changes can eventually affect financial transactions (where redemption proceeds go), but the change itself is non-financial. **Cross-Exam Context:** RTAs handle both financial (purchase, redemption, switch, SIP) and non-financial (address, name, bank, nominee changes) transactions. ISCs accept both types as part of their single-window service. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Encourage clients to keep their KYC details updated. An outdated address or bank mandate can delay redemption proceeds or cause dividend credits to bounce. Proactive updates prevent future headaches.
